core.utils.sampling module

HIGH-FREQUENCY SAMPLING UTILITY

Author: Deepak Panigrahy Institution: Contact:

Module: core/utils/sampling.py (Part of Module 1 - Energy Engine)

Purpose:

This module provides a high-frequency sampling thread that reads RAPL energy counters at a configurable rate (default 100 Hz). This enables:

  1. Capturing energy consumption during short agent steps (Req 1.46)

  2. Attributing energy to specific phases of agentic workflows

  3. Detecting energy spikes from context switches or tool calls

  4. Validating that sampling frequency is sufficient (Req 1.46)

Why a Dedicated Sampling Thread:

  • RAPL counters are cumulative; we need differences over short intervals.

  • A background thread ensures consistent sampling without blocking the main experiment code.

  • Samples are stored with timestamps for later correlation with workflow phases.

Requirements Covered:

Req 1.46 – RAPL Sampling Frequency Validation – ensures we capture enough

samples for short agent steps (default 100 Hz = 10ms intervals).

Design:

  • The sampler runs in a daemon thread so it exits when the main program exits.

  • Samples are stored in a thread-safe queue to avoid locking issues.

  • The main thread can retrieve samples after the measurement stops.

Usage Example:

sampler = HighFrequencySampler(rapl_reader, sampling_rate_hz=100) sampler.start() # … run experiment … sampler.stop() samples = sampler.get_samples()

# Or get samples since a specific timestamp recent = sampler.get_samples_since(start_time)

class core.utils.sampling.EnergySample(timestamp: float, package_uj: int = 0, core_uj: int = 0, dram_uj: int | None = None, uncore_uj: int | None = None, raw_values: Dict[str, int]=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

A single energy sample from the high-frequency sampler.

timestamp

Unix timestamp when the sample was taken.

Type:

float

package_uj

Package energy in microjoules.

Type:

int

core_uj

Core energy in microjoules.

Type:

int

dram_uj

DRAM energy in microjoules (may be None if unavailable).

Type:

int

uncore_uj

Uncore energy in microjoules (may be None).

Type:

int

raw_values

Raw values from all available domains.

Type:

dict

core_uj: int = 0
dram_uj: int | None = None
package_uj: int = 0
raw_values: Dict[str, int]
timestamp: float
to_dict() → Dict[str, Any][source]

Convert sample to dictionary for serialization.

uncore_uj: int | None = None
class core.utils.sampling.HighFrequencySampler(rapl_reader, sampling_rate_hz: int = 100, max_queue_size: int = 10000)[source]

Bases: object

High-frequency RAPL sampling thread.

This class runs a background thread that reads RAPL energy counters at a specified rate (default 100 Hz = every 10ms). Samples are stored in a thread-safe queue for later retrieval.

rapl_reader

RAPLReader instance to read energy counters.

sampling_rate_hz

Target sampling frequency.

Type:

int

interval

Calculated interval between samples.

Type:

float

running

Whether the sampler thread is running.

Type:

bool

sample_queue

Thread-safe queue of EnergySample objects.

Type:

queue.Queue

thread

Background sampler thread.

Type:

threading.Thread

get_samples() → List[EnergySample][source]

Get all samples currently in the queue.

Returns:

List of EnergySample objects.

get_samples_since(timestamp: float) → List[EnergySample][source]

Get all samples taken after a specific timestamp.

Parameters:

timestamp – Unix timestamp (samples after this time are returned).

Returns:

List of EnergySample objects with timestamp > given timestamp.

Note

This method consumes samples from the queue. If you need to keep them, call get_samples() first.

get_statistics() → Dict[str, Any][source]

Get sampling statistics.

Returns:

  • sampling_rate_hz: Target rate

  • interval_ms: Target interval in milliseconds

  • samples_taken: Total samples taken

  • samples_dropped: Samples dropped due to queue full

  • current_queue_size: Current number of samples in queue

Return type:

Dictionary with keys

start() → None[source]

Start the sampling thread.

The thread runs in daemon mode, so it will automatically exit when the main program exits.

start_time: float | None
stop() → List[EnergySample][source]

Stop the sampling thread and return all collected samples.

Returns:

List of EnergySample objects collected during the sampling period.

thread: Thread | None
